---
title: "cc_2026_day8"
author: "Lyndsay Miles"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
30 Day Chart Challenge 2026 Day 8: Circle

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(extrafont)
library(showtext)
library(countrycode)

```

```{r}
#PRESS FREEDOM INDEX RANK. Data retrieved from  https://data360.worldbank.org/en/prosperity
df_pfi <- read.csv("data/RWB_PFI_RANK.csv")
```

```{r}
df_pfi_clean <- df_pfi %>% select(REF_AREA, REF_AREA_LABEL, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE) %>%
  
  rename(
    iso3_code = REF_AREA,
    country   = REF_AREA_LABEL,
    year      = TIME_PERIOD,
    rank      = OBS_VALUE
  )
```

```{r}
df_pfi_clean %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  summarize(
    rank_2002 = rank[year == min(year)],
    rank_2025 = rank[year == max(year)],
    change    = rank_2025 - rank_2002,  # positive = declined (higher rank number = worse)
    pct_change = ((rank_2025 - rank_2002) / rank_2002) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(change))

df_pfi_region <- df_pfi_clean %>%
  filter(!iso3_code %in% c("CSS", "SCG", "XKX", "YUG")) %>%
  mutate(
    region = countrycode(iso3_code,
                         origin      = "iso3c",
                         destination = "region"),
    region = case_when(
      iso3_code == "MEX" ~ "North America",
      TRUE               ~ region
    ),
    is_usa = iso3_code == "USA"
  ) %>%
  mutate(region = gsub("&", "and", region))
```

```{r}
df_pfi_region %>%
  distinct(country, region) %>%
  count(region) 

```

```{r}
#regional average rankings
regional_avg <- df_pfi_region %>%
  group_by(region, year) %>%
  summarize(avg_rank = mean(rank, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")%>%
  mutate(region = gsub("&", "and", region))

```

```{r}
rsf_change <- df_pfi_region %>%
  group_by(country, region) %>%
  summarize(
    rank_start = rank[year == min(year)],
    rank_end   = rank[year == max(year)],
    change     = rank_end - rank_start,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region, desc(change))

print(rsf_change, n = 20)

# Summary by region
rsf_change %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_change    = mean(change),
    most_declined = country[which.max(change)],
    max_decline   = max(change),
    most_improved = country[which.min(change)],
    max_improve   = min(change)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_change))

rsf_change %>% filter(region == "North America")
# Order countries within each region by change (largest first)
rsf_change <- rsf_change %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  mutate(country_order = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(region, desc(change)) %>%
  mutate(
    country_id = row_number(),
    # flag notable countries
    label_flag = case_when(
      country == "United States"        ~ "USA",
      country == "Hong Kong SAR, China" ~ "Hong Kong",
      country == "Nicaragua"            ~ "Nicaragua",
      change >= 80                      ~ country,
      change <= -50                     ~ country,
      TRUE                              ~ ""
    )
  )
```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
font_add_google("Atkinson Hyperlegible", "atkinson")
showtext_auto()

# --- Prep ---
rsf_polar <- rsf_change %>%
  arrange(region, desc(change)) %>%
  mutate(
    change_capped = pmin(change, 100),
    country_id    = row_number(),
    label_flag    = case_when(
  country == "Hong Kong SAR, China" ~ "HKG",
  country == "Nicaragua"            ~ "NIC",
  country == "El Salvador"          ~ "SLV",
  TRUE                              ~ ""
)
  ) %>%
  mutate(region = recode(region,
    "East Asia and Pacific"        = "E. Asia/Pacific",
    "Europe and Central Asia"      = "Europe/C. Asia",
    "Latin America and Caribbean"  = "Latin America",
    "Middle East and North Africa" = "Middle East/N. Africa",
    "Sub-Saharan Africa"           = "Sub-Saharan Africa",
    "South Asia"                   = "South Asia",
    "North America"                = "North America"
  ))

region_labels <- rsf_polar %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarize(
    mid_id = mean(country_id),  # midpoint of region's bars
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    text_color = case_when(
      region %in% c("E. Asia/Pacific", 
                     "Latin America",
                     "North America")  ~ "#2d2d2d",  # dark text on light boxes
      TRUE                             ~ "white"      # white text on dark boxes
    )
  )

# --- Plot ---
ggplot(rsf_polar, aes(x = country_id, y = change_capped, fill = region)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_text(
    data = rsf_polar %>% 
      filter(label_flag != "") %>%
      mutate(
        nudge_y = case_when(
          label_flag == "SLV" ~ change_capped + 15,
          TRUE                ~ change_capped + 5
        )
      ),
    aes(label = label_flag, y = nudge_y),
    size   = 2.8,
    family = "atkinson",
    color  = "#2d2d2d",
    hjust  = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data          = region_labels,
    aes(x         = mid_id, y = 108, label = region,
        fill      = region,
        color     = text_color),
    size          = 2.5,
    family        = "atkinson",
    fontface      = "bold",
    label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),
    label.r       = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    label.size    = 0
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#2d2d2d", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.5) +
  coord_polar(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(-5, 115),
    breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Which Countries Have Lost the Most Press Freedom?",
    subtitle = "Change in Press Freedom Index rank 2002–2025\nLonger bars = greater loss of press freedom",
    caption  = "Note: Bars capped at 100 (Nicaragua +138, Hong Kong +122, El Salvador +101)\nSource: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) Press Freedom Index\nCreated by: Lyndsay Miles | #30DayChartChallenge Day 8",
    fill     = NULL,
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    aspect.ratio          = 1,
    text                  = element_text(family = "atkinson"),
    plot.title            = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5,
                                         margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.subtitle         = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0.5,
                                         margin = margin(b = 16)),
    plot.caption          = element_text(size = 7, color = "#2d2d2d", hjust = 0,
                                         margin = margin(t = 12)),
    plot.title.position   = "plot",
    plot.caption.position = "plot",
    plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "#f2f0ed", color = NA),
    panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "#f2f0ed", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major      = element_line(color = "#d8d8d8", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor      = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing         = unit(0, "cm"),
    axis.text.x           = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y           = element_text(size = 7, color = "gray50",
                                         family = "atkinson"),
    legend.position       = "none",
    plot.margin           = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )
```
Day 8 of the #30DayChartChallenge (Circular) 🌐
A starburst chart showing which countries have lost the most press freedom between 2002 and 2025, grouped by region. Each bar = one country. Longer bar = bigger decline.
Hong Kong, Nicaragua, and El Salvador all lost more than 100 places in rank.
Data: Reporters Without Borders Press Freedom Index
